rationale: Cardano uses a reserve-based emission model where staking rewards are drawn from a fixed reserve of ~14B ADA (originally 45B max supply minus circulating); each epoch (~5 days) releases 0.3% of the remaining reserve, causing absolute emissions to shrink geometrically over time. As of mid-2025, circulating supply is approximately 35.9B ADA out of a 45B hard cap, with the remaining reserve (~9.1B ADA) decaying at ~0.3%/epoch, yielding an annualized new-issuance rate of roughly 2.6–2.8% of current circulating supply — a rate that declines continuously as the reserve depletes.
